title: The host in Seoul who waited up for me
summary: A short story about a homestay host who stayed awake to make sure you got home safely.
topic: The host in Seoul who waited up for me
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about a host, friend, or family member who waited up late for you in another country. Describe the night and how their kindness made you feel. Use past simple and past continuous.
hintStarters: On my second night in…, I went out with…
When I finally got back to the apartment…
I tried to apologise, but she only said…
sampleText: On my second night in Seoul, I went out with three students I had met that morning at a language exchange. We ate spicy stew, then walked along a small river, and somehow it became one in the morning. I had forgotten that my homestay host, Mrs Park, locked the front door at eleven. When I finally got back to the apartment, I tiptoed up the stairs and tried to use my key as quietly as possible. The door opened before I touched it. Mrs Park was standing in the hallway in her grey cardigan, holding a small lamp. She did not look angry, only tired. 'I was a little worried,' she said in slow English. On the kitchen table there was a cup of warm barley tea and a blanket folded over a chair. She had been waiting for me for almost two hours. I tried to apologise, but she only said, 'Sit, drink, then sleep.' While I was drinking the tea, she went quietly back to her room. I felt embarrassed but also strangely held, like a child again. I still write to Mrs Park every winter, and I have never once been late for anyone since.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Tôi đi chơi với ba sinh viên

sampleAnnotations.phrase: locked the front door at eleven
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: khóa cửa trước lúc mười một giờ
Tôi rón rén lên cầu thang

sampleAnnotations.phrase: standing in the hallway in her grey cardigan
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: đang đứng ở hành lang trong chiếc áo len xám

sampleAnnotations.phrase: a cup of warm barley tea
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một cốc trà lúa mạch ấm

sampleAnnotations.phrase: She had been waiting for me for almost two hours
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Bà đã đợi tôi gần hai tiếng đồng hồ

sampleAnnotations.phrase: While I was drinking the tea
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Trong khi tôi đang uống trà

sampleAnnotations.phrase: strangely held, like a child again
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: được ôm ấp lạ kỳ, như một đứa trẻ lần nữa

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past perfect for an action before another past action
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'I had forgotten that my host locked the door at eleven' dùng past perfect ('had forgotten') vì việc quên xảy ra trước khoảnh khắc tôi về nhà (past simple).
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'I had forgotten that my host locked the door at eleven' uses past perfect ('had forgotten') because the forgetting happened before the past moment of arriving home (past simple).

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past continuous for ongoing background
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'While I was drinking the tea, she went quietly back to her room' — past continuous mô tả nền, past simple mô tả hành động chen vào.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'While I was drinking the tea, she went quietly back to her room' — past continuous sets the background; past simple marks the inserted action.

sampleTranslationVi: Vào đêm thứ hai của tôi ở Seoul, tôi đi chơi với ba sinh viên mà tôi đã gặp sáng hôm đó ở một buổi trao đổi ngôn ngữ. Chúng tôi ăn món hầm cay, sau đó đi dọc một con sông nhỏ, và không hiểu sao đã thành một giờ sáng. Tôi đã quên rằng bà chủ nhà của tôi, bà Park, khóa cửa trước lúc mười một giờ. Khi cuối cùng tôi về đến căn hộ, tôi rón rén lên cầu thang và cố dùng chìa khóa nhẹ nhàng nhất có thể. Cửa mở ra trước khi tôi chạm vào. Bà Park đang đứng ở hành lang trong chiếc áo len xám, cầm một chiếc đèn nhỏ. Bà không có vẻ giận, chỉ mệt. 'Tôi hơi lo một chút,' bà nói bằng tiếng Anh chậm rãi. Trên bàn bếp có một cốc trà lúa mạch ấm và một chiếc chăn gấp trên ghế. Bà đã đợi tôi gần hai tiếng đồng hồ. Tôi cố xin lỗi, nhưng bà chỉ nói, 'Ngồi, uống, rồi ngủ.' Trong khi tôi đang uống trà, bà lặng lẽ quay về phòng. Tôi cảm thấy xấu hổ nhưng cũng được ôm ấp lạ kỳ, như một đứa trẻ lần nữa. Tôi vẫn viết thư cho bà Park mỗi mùa đông, và tôi chưa bao giờ đi trễ với ai kể từ ngày đó.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past simple opening.
options: On my second night in Seoul, I went out with three students.
On my second night in Seoul, I am going out with three students.
On my second night in Seoul, I have gone out with three students.
On my second night in Seoul, I go out with three students.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'went' for a finished past night.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses past perfect?
options: I had forgotten that my host locked the door at eleven.
I have forgotten that my host locked the door at eleven.
I forgot that my host had locking the door at eleven.
I forgetting that my host locked the door at eleven.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past perfect 'had forgotten' marks an event before another past event.

options: stay awake until someone arrives
stand up while waiting
wait outside the door
call someone on the phone
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Wait up' = stay awake late for someone.
mcQuestions.hint: Don't go to sleep until they come.

options: I tiptoed up the stairs.
I was tiptoe up the stairs.
I tiptoeing up the stairs.
I have tiptoe up the stairs.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'tiptoed' for a finished quiet movement.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct preposition: 'standing ___ the hallway'.
options: in
on
at
by
mcQuestions.explanation: 'In the hallway' — inside an enclosed space.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses 'while' + past continuous?
options: While I was drinking the tea, she went back to her room.
While I drink the tea, she went back to her room.
While I am drinking the tea, she went back to her room.
While I have drink the tea, she went back to her room.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'While + past continuous' sets the background; past simple marks the inserted action.

options: feeling uncomfortable because you did something wrong or awkward
feeling very tired
feeling very angry
feeling very hungry

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct sentence about duration.
options: She had been waiting for me for almost two hours.
She has been waiting me for almost two hours.
She was waited for me since almost two hours.
She is waiting me since almost two hours.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past perfect continuous 'had been waiting + for + duration' is natural here.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence with reported speech.
options: She said in slow English, 'I was a little worried.'
She said to slow English, 'I was a little worried.'
She told in slow English that 'I was a little worried.'
She say in slow English, 'I was a little worried.'
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Said in (a language)' is the natural collocation when quoting directly.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does 'as quietly as possible' mean?
options: in the quietest way one can manage
louder than usual
at the same time as someone
without thinking
mcQuestions.explanation: 'As + adverb + as possible' = the maximum possible degree of that quality.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the most natural closing sentence.
options: I have never once been late for anyone since.
I never once was late for anyone since.
I never have been once late for anyone since.
I am never once late for anyone since.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present perfect 'have never been' + 'since' for an unbroken span up to now.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do small acts of waiting — a parent, a host, a friend — often feel heavier than larger gifts? What does waiting up actually communicate?
